## 🎉 First release — `things-cli` v0.1.0

A command-line interface for [Things3](https://culturedcode.com/things/) on macOS. Reads tasks, projects, areas and tags directly from the Things3 SQLite database (read-only) and writes via the official `things:///` URL scheme and AppleScript — so Things stays the source of truth and your data never leaves the machine.

### Highlights

- **Read anything** — `things today`, `things inbox`, `things upcoming`, project/area/tag filters, full-text `things search`, and `things show` with numeric references into the last list
- **Write safely** — `things add` creates tasks via the Things URL scheme; `things complete` / `things cancel` / `things log` drive Things via AppleScript
- **JSON everywhere** — `-j` on any command emits structured output for piping into `jq`
- **Pure Go** — single static binary, no cgo, no external SQLite dependency (`modernc.org/sqlite`)
- **Installable three ways**
  - `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ryanlewis/things-cli/main/install.sh | sh` (verifies checksums)
  - `go install github.com/ryanlewis/things-cli/cmd/things@latest`
  - Download a tarball from this release and drop the binary onto your `PATH`

### Requirements

macOS with Things3 installed. Binaries for Apple Silicon (`darwin_arm64`) and Intel (`darwin_amd64`).
